meaning: adj.顽童似的,似顽童的,顽皮的
examples: Garcia is well known for his impish sense of humour.
I first met her in 1974, when she was a slightly impish 21-year-old home from Oxford on summer vacation.
"This can also earn me some pocket money," Gu says with an impish smile, "as the stock I bought has gone down."
In this series of commercials, each impish man transgresses an established gender norm.
That last picture shows him as I'll always remember him, his eyes sparkling and with a wide, impish grin.
